title: Nx Cloud tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Nx Cloud access token detected in variable assignment. Nx Cloud access tokens provide authentication to distributed caching and remote task execution services for monorepo builds. Exposing these tokens allows unauthorized access to build caches, potentially poisoning cached artifacts, consuming compute resources, and accessing sensitive build information. Store tokens securely using environment variables or secret management systems.

Nx Cloud access token detected in nx.json configuration. Hardcoded Nx Cloud tokens in nx.json or configuration files expose distributed build cache credentials. This allows unauthorized users to access cached build artifacts, inject malicious code into the cache, consume expensive compute resources, and compromise build integrity. Use environment variable substitution or CI secret injection instead.

Nx Cloud token detected in CLI command. Nx CLI commands with --cloud flag expose access tokens used for distributed caching and remote execution. These tokens grant access to build caches and task execution resources. Exposing them allows unauthorized cache access, cache poisoning attacks, and abuse of compute resources. Use environment variables or CI secret injection.

Nx Cloud bearer token detected in authorization header. Bearer tokens in authorization headers provide direct API access to Nx Cloud services including distributed caching, remote task execution, and build analytics. Exposing these tokens allows unauthorized API access, cache manipulation, and potential compromise of build artifacts. Store tokens securely and rotate immediately if exposed.

Generic long authentication token detected in Nx Cloud context. Long alphanumeric tokens (40-80 characters) in Nx-related code often represent access tokens for Nx Cloud distributed caching and remote execution services. Exposing these allows unauthorized cache access, potential cache poisoning, compute resource abuse, and build artifact compromise. Verify token type and rotate immediately.
